scenario: opencode adapter validate(component, changes, ctx) → Diagnostic[] for every recognized file in tests/fixtures/bundles/opencode/. Mirrors lint/all-types.json's perType shape. SPECIFICATION golden.
adapterStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED — spec-authored target (future Chunk-2 format-adapter work item).
specSource: docs/design/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-spec.md §6 (command/agent `missing-name-or-type`; skill `missing-skill-md` + Agent Skills contract §4.5), §2/§5.1 (Diagnostic {file, issue, detail, fixed}).
realWorldSource: https://opencode.ai/docs/commands/ , https://opencode.ai/docs/agents/ , https://opencode.ai/docs/skills/ — the frontmatter fields (description/agent/model/mode/tools) the opencode adapter tolerates
notes: SPECIFICATION golden — no opencode adapter exists yet. Every fixture file is CONFORMANT → []. The opencode adapter, like claude, is a tool-dir translator and tolerates OpenCode's documented frontmatter fields. The strict-unknown-frontmatter behavior belongs to `agent-skills`, not to the tool-dir adapters (spec §6 skill row).
The changelog skill has a valid SKILL.md with name (charset OK, == dir) + description (< 1024) → no `missing-skill-md`, no Agent Skills hard-rule violations.

The `command` perType entry represents both the canonical plural commands/test.md AND the singular-dir alias command/legacy.md (open question 6 RESOLVED: both dir forms accepted). Validation keys on the derived `type`, not the dir spelling, so both are conformant → [].

command.note: conformant: has $ARGUMENTS body probe + description/agent frontmatter.

agent.note: conformant: description present; mode/model/temperature/tools are OpenCode fields → tolerated.

skill.note: SKILL.md present; name/description valid → no diagnostics.
